message.content: Read /Users/gbang/Downloads/algora/frontend/app.js and /Users/gbang/Downloads/algora/frontend/styles.css (and /Users/gbang/Downloads/algora/frontend/markdown.js if present). Determine: (1) the exact JSON shape of a tool block the frontend expects inside a saved assistant turn (the write_file path/content and run_command command contract), (2) how the inline tabbed "Complete Code Implementation" full-code viewer works and when it shows, (3) the full conversation JSON shape the frontend loads (messages[] + transcript[] + blocks[]), (4) how "lld" mode is detected and rendered differently from other modes. Quote key field names.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
30	    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
31	        return None
32	
33	
34	def save(session_id: str, mode: str, title: str, messages: list, transcript: list) -> None:
35	    """Write/overwrite the full conversation. `transcript` is the COMPLETE list."""
36	    config.CONV_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
37	    existing = load(session_id)
38	    created = existing.get("created_at") if existing else time.time()
39	    data = {
40	        "session_id": session_id,
41	        "mode": mode,
42	        "title": (title or "Untitled").strip()[:120],
43	        "created_at": created,
44	        "updated_at": time.time(),
45	        "messages": messages,
46	        "transcript": transcript,
47	    }
48	    tmp = _path(session_id).with_suffix(".json.tmp")
49	    # default=str so one unexpected (non-serializable) block never loses the
50	    # whole turn's history.
51	    tmp.write_text(json.dumps(data, default=str), encoding="utf-8")
52	    tmp.replace(_path(session_id))  # atomic
53	
54	
55	def list_all() -> list[dict]:
56	    """Metadata for every saved conversation, newest first."""
57	    if not config.CONV_DIR.exists():
58	        return []
59	    out = []
60	    for p in config.CONV_DIR.glob("*.json"):
61	        try:
62	            d = json.loads(p.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
63	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
64	            continue
65	        out.append(
66	            {
67	                "session_id": d.get("session_id"),
68	                "mode": d.get("mode"),
69	                "title": d.get("title", "Untitled"),
70	                "updated_at": d.get("updated_at", 0),
71	                "turns": sum(1 for t in d.get("transcript", []) if t.get("role") == "user"),
72	            }
73	        )
74	    out.sort(key=lambda x: x.get("updated_at", 0), reverse=True)
75	    return out
76	
77	
78	def delete(session_id: str) -> bool:
79	    p = _path(session_id)
80	    if p.exists():
81	        p.unlink()
82	        return True
83	    return False
84	
85	
86	# --- Transcript builder: coalesces the streamed event dicts into render-ready items ---
87	
88	
89	class TranscriptBuilder:
90	    """Consumes the same event dicts the server streams and produces a compact,
91	    render-ready transcript for one user turn: [user_item, assistant_item]."""
92	
93	    def __init__(self) -> None:
94	        self._user: dict | None = None
95	        self._blocks: list[dict] = []
96	        self._text = ""
97	        self._thinking = ""
98	        self._tool_by_id: dict[str, dict] = {}
99	        self._usage: dict | None = None
100	
101	    def add_user(self, user_content) -> None:
102	        text, images = "", 0
103	        if isinstance(user_content, list):
104	            for b in user_content:
105	                if not isinstance(b, dict):
106	                    continue
107	                if b.get("type") == "text":
108	                    text = b.get("text", "")
109	                elif b.get("type") == "image":
110	                    images += 1
111	        elif isinstance(user_content, str):
112	            text = user_content
113	        self._user = {"role": "user", "text": text, "images": images}
114	
115	    def _flush_text(self) -> None:
116	        if self._text.strip():
117	            self._blocks.append({"k": "text", "md": self._text})
118	        self._text = ""
119	
120	    def consume(self, ev: dict) -> None:
121	        t = ev.get("type")
122	        if t == "step_start":
123	            self._flush_text()
124	        elif t == "thinking_delta":
125	            self._thinking += ev.get("text", "")
126	        elif t == "text_delta":
127	            self._text += ev.get("text", "")
128	        elif t == "web_search":
129	            self._flush_text()
130	            self._blocks.append({"k": "web", "query": ev.get("query")})
131	        elif t == "tool_call":
132	            self._flush_text()
133	            block = {
134	                "k": "tool",
135	                "id": ev.get("id"),
136	                "name": ev.get("name"),
137	                "input": ev.get("input"),
138	                "output": None,
139	                "is_error": False,
140	            }
141	            self._blocks.append(block)
142	            self._tool_by_id[ev.get("id")] = block
143	        elif t == "tool_result":
144	            block = self._tool_by_id.get(ev.get("id"))
145	            if block is not None:
146	                block["output"] = ev.get("output")
147	                block["is_error"] = bool(ev.get("is_error"))
148	        elif t == "turn_done":
149	            self._flush_text()
150	            self._usage = ev.get("usage")
151	
152	    def items(self) -> list[dict]:
153	        self._flush_text()
154	        assistant = {
155	            "role": "assistant",
156	            "thinking": self._thinking,
157	            "blocks": self._blocks,
158	            "usage": self._usage,
159	        }
160	        out = []
161	        if self._user:
162	            out.append(self._user)
163	        out.append(assistant)
164	        return out

content.text: I now have the complete picture, confirmed against both the frontend consumer (app.js) and the server-side producer (store.py). I have everything I need to answer all four questions precisely.

input.toolBlockContract:
A tool block lives inside a saved assistant turn's `blocks[]` array. The frontend's discriminant field is `k` (in `renderSavedAssistant`, app.js ~lines 985-998), with three values: `"text"`, `"tool"`, and `"web"`. A `k:"tool"` block has these exact fields: `id` (string; if absent the frontend synthesizes `"t" + random`), `name` (the tool name string), `input` (object), `output` (string), and `is_error` (boolean, coerced via `!!b.is_error`). On restore the frontend replays it by calling `addToolCall(tab, {id, name: b.name, input: b.input || {}})` then `addToolResult(tab, {id, name: b.name, output: b.output || "", is_error: !!b.is_error})`.

The per-tool `input` contract is defined in `targetForTool(name, inp)` (app.js ~lines 355-360) and `addToolCall` (~362-399):
- write_file: `input.path` (string filename) and `input.content` (string file body). `path` becomes `tool-target`; `content` is rendered as a code block AND captured into `turn.files` as `{path, content}` (dedup by path, last write wins) which powers the full-code viewer. TOOL_META key `write_file` -> cls "write", icon "✎".
- run_command: `input.command` (string). `path` is NOT used; `command` becomes `tool-target` and is shown as `$ <command>` in a `<pre>`. TOOL_META key `run_command` -> cls "run", icon "$".
- read_file / run_python: also key off `input.path`. run_python additionally reads `input.stdin` (rendered under a "stdin" io-label).
- list_files: no target field used.

This exactly matches the server producer in backend/store.py `TranscriptBuilder.consume()` which emits `{"k":"tool","id":...,"name":...,"input":...,"output":None,"is_error":False}` and later fills `output`/`is_error` from the matching tool_result event. The `web` block is `{"k":"web","query": <string>}`. The `text` block is `{"k":"text","md": <markdown string>}`.

Live (streaming) tool events use a parallel shape (not blocks): `tool_call` event = `{type, id, name, input}`; `tool_result` event = `{type, id, name, output, is_error}`.
input.viewerBehavior:
There are TWO distinct full-code viewers, both fed from the SAME data source `turn.files` (an array of `{path, content}` captured during streaming from every write_file tool_call, deduped by path with last-write-wins — see addToolCall app.js ~376-382). Dispatch happens in `attachFullCode(turn, mode)` (app.js ~718-729), called on `turn_done` and on restore. (A) The INLINE tabbed "Complete Code Implementation" viewer — `renderInlineFullCode(turn)` (app.js ~735-801). This is the hellointerview-style viewer the question asks about. It builds a `
` appended to `turn.body`, containing: - `.ifc-head` with `

Complete Code Implementation

` and an `.ifc-copyall` "copy all" button. - `.ifc-blurb` static text ("While most companies only require pseudocode... Below is the complete working implementation, file by file."). - `.ifc-viewer` containing `.ifc-tabs` (role="tablist") and a single `.ifc-pane`. Each file becomes a `
input.conversationJsonShape:
A loaded conversation (GET /api/conversations/<id>, consumed in restoreConversation app.js ~1005-1041 and produced by backend/store.py `save()`) is a JSON object with top-level keys:
- `session_id` (string, encoded as "<uuid>:<mode>")
- `mode` (string: assessment | interview | lld | hld | behavioral)
- `title` (string, trimmed to 120 chars, defaults "Untitled")
- `created_at`, `updated_at` (epoch seconds)
- `messages[]` — the raw API message log (the model's own conversation history; NOT what the UI renders directly)
- `transcript[]` — the coalesced, render-ready view the UI actually replays.

`transcript[]` is a flat list of role items. Each item has `role` ("user" or "assistant"):
USER item: `{role:"user", text:<string>, images:<int count>}`. The frontend renders it via addUserMessage, prefixing `📎 <n> image(s)\n` when `images` is truthy.
ASSISTANT item: `{role:"assistant", thinking:<string>, blocks:[...], usage:<obj|null>}`. renderSavedAssistant: if `item.thinking` is set it renders a collapsed reasoning block; then iterates `item.blocks || []`; finally `attachFullCode` and `showUsage(item.usage)`.

`blocks[]` (discriminated by `k`):
- `{k:"text", md:<markdown>}` -> rendered through renderMarkdown into a `.prose` div.
- `{k:"tool", id, name, input, output, is_error}` -> replayed as a tool card + result.
- `{k:"web", query}` -> a web-search card.

The LIST endpoint (GET /api/conversations?mode=<mode>) returns `{conversations:[...]}` where each entry is metadata only: `{session_id, mode, title, updated_at, turns}` — `turns` = count of user items in the transcript. The frontend computes a change signature from `session_id:updated_at:turns` to poll-refresh history without repainting. `usage` (when present) has `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, optional `cache_read_input_tokens` (showUsage app.js ~466-474).
input.modeHandling:
Mode is one of five values held in the `tabs` object and on each tab as `tab.mode`: `assessment`, `interview`, `lld`, `hld`, `behavioral` (app.js ~73-79; PLACEHOLDERS ~52-58). The active mode is also encoded into the session id as the suffix after a colon: `newSessionId(mode)` = `rawId() + ":" + mode`, and on load the mode is recovered with `urlSession.split(":").pop()` (~1204). The conversation JSON also carries `mode` at top level, used by restoreConversation to switch tabs.

The single place mode changes RENDERING is `attachFullCode(turn, mode)` (app.js ~718-729). The check is literally `if (mode === "lld")`:
- lld mode: hide the `.full-code-btn` (`btn.hidden = true`) and instead call `renderInlineFullCode(turn)` to inject the inline tabbed "Complete Code Implementation" section directly into the answer body. The code comment states "In LLD mode we render a hellointerview-style 'Complete Code Implementation' section INLINE at the end of the answer — collapsible per file, no modal. In every other mode the existing 📂 full code button reveals the modal."
- all other modes (assessment/interview/hld/behavioral): leave the inline section off and set `btn.hidden = !hasFiles`, so the `📂 full code` button (opening the modal) appears only if files were written.

This dispatch runs identically on both the live `turn_done` path (handleEvent ~508) and the restore/live-sync path (renderSavedAssistant ~1001), so saved lld conversations re-render the inline viewer too. Beyond full-code dispatch, mode also selects: the composer placeholder text (PLACEHOLDERS), and the per-mode follow-up `QUICK_ACTIONS` button set (app.js ~298-333; lld's set is Design Patterns / Sequence Diagram / Error Handling / Unit Tests). The CSS for the inline viewer (`.inline-fullcode`, `.ifc-*`) is documented in styles.css ~871-965 as "(LLD mode only)". Note also the separate LP Coach tab (`lp-tab`) is a special iframe embed that bypasses the session/streaming system entirely and is not part of the five modes.

content.text:
I've analyzed all four files and cross-checked the contract against the server-side producer. Here's what I found.

Relevant files:
- `/Users/gbang/Downloads/algora/frontend/app.js` (consumer)
- `/Users/gbang/Downloads/algora/frontend/markdown.js` (`renderCodeBlock`, `renderMarkdown`)
- `/Users/gbang/Downloads/algora/frontend/styles.css` (`.inline-fullcode` / `.ifc-*`, `.code-modal` / `.cm-*`)
- `/Users/gbang/Downloads/algora/backend/store.py` (producer — `TranscriptBuilder` + `save()`, confirms field names)

Summary of the four answers:

1. Tool block contract — blocks are discriminated by `k`. A `k:"tool"` block = `{id, name, input, output, is_error}`. `write_file`'s `input` uses `path` + `content` (content is captured into `turn.files` as `{path, content}`, dedup by path, last-write-wins). `run_command`'s `input` uses `command` (not `path`). Confirmed identical in store.py's `TranscriptBuilder.consume()`.

2. Inline "Complete Code Implementation" viewer — `renderInlineFullCode(turn)` builds `<section class="inline-fullcode">` with `.ifc-tabs` (one `.ifc-tab` per file, showing path + line count) and a single fixed-height `.ifc-pane` that swaps content on tab click. Shows only when `mode === "lld"` and files exist. Non-lld modes use the modal (`openCodeModal`) behind the `📂 full code` button.

3. Conversation JSON — top level `{session_id, mode, title, created_at, updated_at, messages[], transcript[]}`. `transcript[]` is flat role items: user = `{role:"user", text, images}`, assistant = `{role:"assistant", thinking, blocks[], usage}`. `blocks[]` discriminated by `k` (`text`/`tool`/`web`). The list endpoint returns `{conversations:[{session_id, mode, title, updated_at, turns}]}`.

4. lld mode — detected via `tab.mode` (and the `:<mode>` session-id suffix / top-level `mode`). The one rendering branch is `if (mode === "lld")` in `attachFullCode`: lld renders the inline section and hides the button; every other mode shows the `📂 full code` modal button only when files exist.
